text:
Once upon a time in a quaint village nestled between rolling hills and tranquil rivers, there lived an old witch known for her peculiar ways. Her name was Elfrida. She lived in a thatched cottage by the riverbank, where the scent of wildflowers often mingled with the aroma of her mystical brews.

Elfrida was famously rumored to have the power to turn stones into pudding and to make flowers sing. While not everyone believed her abilities, her cottage was nonetheless a place of peace, buzzing with the energy of nature and whimsy.

One serene morning, as dawn painted the sky in hues of pink and gold, Elfrida decided to brew her special sunrise tea. The recipe was simple: a teaspoon of dew-kissed rose petals, a sprinkle of fairy-gold thyme, and a whole lot of warm milk. As she worked, she hummed a tune that seemed to dance over the water.

The concoction was ready soon after. She poured herself a cup
